theme: project plan robust to subsystem-wide delays
title: Subsystem-Robust Release Schedule
strategies: own-duration baseline: rank each job by its own nominal duration alone (descending), ignoring both downstream chain length and every published perturbation

nominal critical-path list scheduling: rank jobs by longest-remaining-nominal-path (tail length) and greedily list-schedule -- the standard textbook recipe for P|prec|Cmax, blind to which subsystem each job belongs to

worst-case-tail list scheduling: replace each job's weight by its worst published inflation before propagating tail lengths through the DAG, so priority reflects what a job could cost under the sweep, not just its nominal cost -- keeps subsystems that could become a long chain from also being queued behind 'safe' long-but-never-perturbed work
